meta.name: List properties instantly with UploadToURL, OpenAI Vision, WordPress and Airtable

parameters.content:
## List properties instantly with UploadToURL, OpenAI Vision, and WordPress
The Problem: Real estate agents lose hours manually uploading property photos, writing descriptions, and syncing data between their website and internal MLS.
The Solution: An "upload-and-forget" pipeline that hosts photos via UploadToURL, uses Vision AI to write professional listings, and publishes to WordPress and Airtable in parallel.

⚙️ How it Works
Webhook: Receives a property photo (Binary or URL) and address metadata.

UploadToURL: Hosts the file and returns a public CDN link for the listing.

GPT-4o Vision: Analyzes the room type, condition, and features to write a description.

Parallel Publish: Simultaneously creates a WordPress draft and an Airtable MLS record.

🔐 Credentials & Setup
Node: Install n8n-nodes-uploadtourl via Community Nodes.

APIs: UploadToURL, OpenAI (Vision), WordPress, and Airtable.

Variables: Set WP_BASE_URL and AIRTABLE_BASE_ID

## 1 — Upload & Vision analysis

**Webhook → Validate → Has URL? → Upload to URL (×2) → Extract CDN URL → GPT-4o Vision → Parse Vision**

Validates `listingId`, `address`, and file type. UploadToURL hosts via the native community node. Vision prompt requests MLS-grade output: room type, 1–10 condition score, pricing signals, feature tags, and a ready-to-publish room description.

## 2 — Parallel publish

**WordPress Upsert + Airtable Upsert (run simultaneously)**

Both branches receive the same enriched payload and run in parallel. WordPress creates or updates a draft post keyed on `listingId` in post meta. Airtable creates or patches the MLS record keyed on `Listing ID`. Neither branch waits for the other — both feed into the merge node.

## 3 — Merge & notify

**Merge → Build Response → Telegram → Respond to Webhook**

A Merge node (mode: combine) waits for both WordPress and Airtable to complete, then assembles a unified response with both platform IDs and URLs. Telegram sends the agent a message with links to both the WordPress draft and the Airtable MLS record.

values.content: You are a licensed real estate appraiser and MLS listing specialist. Your job is to analyse property photos and produce structured, MLS-compliant descriptions. Return ONLY a valid JSON object — no markdown, no preamble.

nodes.name: Parse Vision & Build Payloads
nodes.notes: Parses Vision JSON and pre-builds both platform payloads in one pass: WordPress Gutenberg block content and the complete Airtable record object. Both parallel branches just reference these pre-built fields.

nodes.name: WordPress - Create Draft Post
nodes.notes: Creates a WordPress draft post with Gutenberg block content. All property meta fields are written as post meta for use with a real estate theme or plugin like WP All Import.

nodes.name: Airtable - Create MLS Record
nodes.notes: Creates the MLS record in Airtable simultaneously with the WordPress post. Uses autoMapInputData from the pre-built airtableRecord object in Parse Vision node.

nodes.name: Merge Platform Results
nodes.notes: Waits for both WordPress and Airtable to complete before proceeding. Set to 'combine' mode so it only fires once both inputs have data.
